subject: DevOps
title: Merge Early, Merge Often
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Merge Early, Merge Often.

hook.text: Two developers worked on the same file for two weeks. The merge conflict took three days to untangle.

buildup.text: Long branches drift apart. The longer they live, the harder it is to bring them back together.

discovery.text: CI says: merge to main at least once a day. Small merges create small conflicts — or none at all.

twist.text: Frequent merging also reveals integration bugs early. Two features that work alone may clash together.

climax.text: Trunk-based development takes it further: everyone commits directly to main with short-lived branches.

punchline.text: Merge daily. Small conflicts beat big merge nightmares.

quiz.question: Why does CI recommend merging to main at least once a day?
options: To keep merge conflicts small and catch integration issues early
To reduce the number of Git branches visible in the UI
To make the commit history look cleaner
